case.title: MSA, Identity Heatmap, and Phylogeny
case.workflow: Compare a small reporter family panel to see what is conserved, what is engineered, and what is genuinely distant.

records.name: EGFP_CDS
records.origin: Engineered fluorescent reporter derived from the Aequorea victoria GFP family.
records.why_it_matters: EGFP is a canonical lab reporter: great for teaching translation, cloning, sequence verification, and how a few codons can change an optical phenotype.

records.name: EGFP_Y67H_training_variant
records.origin: Training derivative of EGFP that changes the aromatic residue in the chromophore-forming motif.
records.why_it_matters: This is not a random mutation. It models the idea that one codon change near a chromophore can produce a large optical shift, which is a powerful lesson for variant interpretation.
records.source_label: Derived from EGFP mutant logic described in GFP engineering literature

records.name: EGFP_S204Y_training_variant
records.origin: Training derivative of EGFP that alters an aromatic-packing site near the chromophore environment.
records.why_it_matters: It gives the tutorial a second closely related variant, which makes alignment, consensus, and comparison-lens examples far more realistic than comparing unrelated proteins only.

records.name: mCherry_CDS
records.origin: Monomeric red fluorescent protein from the mFruit engineering lineage.
records.why_it_matters: mCherry is a real-world counterexample to GFP: same broad job as a reporter, different sequence history, color, and engineering constraints.
records.source_label: PubMed: A monomeric red fluorescent protein
